reasons: Diggie directly reduces Aurora's freeze-based teamfight value.
Time Journey can protect nearby allies during Aurora's main control window.

proof.summary: Aurora wants to freeze multiple enemies and burst them, while Diggie can cleanse debuffs and grant control immunity to nearby allies.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Aurora's real freeze combo.
Teammates stay close enough to receive protection.
failureCases: Aurora baits Diggie's ultimate before committing.

reasons: Kagura has a built-in debuff removal and dash to escape control timing.
She can poke Aurora from angles without fully committing into freeze range.

proof.summary: Aurora wants to catch enemies with freeze timing, while Kagura can remove debuffs, dash, and reposition before counter-poking.
worksBestWhen: Kagura keeps her cleanse available for Aurora's freeze setup.
Kagura avoids standing near grouped teammates.
failureCases: Kagura uses mobility before Aurora commits.
Aurora triggers Kagura after baiting her cleanse.

reasons: Lancelot can dodge key Aurora skill timing with invulnerable or untargetable frames.
He can punish Aurora after her freeze combo or passive window is forced.

proof.summary: Aurora wants reliable freeze timing, while Lancelot can avoid important damage and control windows and re-enter after cooldowns.
worksBestWhen: Lancelot waits for Aurora's freeze skill to be committed.
Lancelot can force Aurora's passive before the main fight.
failureCases: Lancelot mistimes immunity and gets frozen.
Aurora stays protected behind frontline control.

reasons: Franco can pick Aurora before she sets up teamfight control.
Suppression helps force Aurora out of position and lets teammates follow up after her passive is triggered.

proof.summary: Aurora is dangerous when she controls the fight from safe range, while Franco can punish poor positioning with hook or suppression.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches Aurora before she has full team protection.
Teammates wait out or re-engage after Aurora's passive.
failureCases: Franco hooks a tank instead of Aurora.
Aurora freezes Franco's team during the attempted pickoff.

reasons: Kaja can isolate Aurora before she controls a teamfight.
Suppression gives a reliable pickoff tool if Aurora is exposed.

proof.summary: Aurora wants to cast from a protected position, while Kaja can suppress and drag her into a burst window.
worksBestWhen: Kaja flanks or Flicker-engages onto Aurora.
The team is ready to finish after Aurora's passive is forced.
failureCases: Aurora's team peels immediately after the drag.
